{{aimai}}

'''若一王子'''は、[[和歌山県]]の[[神社]]。
*和歌山県[[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」
[[Category:修験道の神|にゃくいちおうし]]
{{aimai}}

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、[[和歌山県]]の[[神社]]。
*和歌山県[[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」
[[Category:修験道の神|にゃくいちおうし]]
{{aimai}}

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、日本の[[神社]]。
*和歌山県[[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」 *長野県大町市の[[若一王子神社]]。
[[Category:修験道の神|にゃくいちおうし]]
{{aimai}}

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、日本の[[神社]]。
*[[東京都]][[北区]]の[[王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。
[[Category:修験道の神|にゃくいちおうし]]
{{aimai}}

*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、日本の[[神社]]。
*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。

revision.comment: 若一王子は「神社」ではなく「神」
revision.text:
'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、[[神仏習合]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。'''若王子'''（にゃくおうじ）ともいう。
[[熊野三山]]に祀られる[[熊野権現|熊野十二所権現]]は三所権現・五所王子・四所明神に分けられ、若一王子は五所王子の第一位である。若一王子の本地仏は[[十一面観音]]で、[[天照大神]]あるいは[[ニニギ]]と同一視された。[[熊野本宮大社]]・[[熊野速玉大社]]では第4殿、[[熊野那智大社]]では第5殿に祀られる（いずれも、現在は「若宮」と称し、天照大神のこととしている）。
[[熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、熊野権現が各地に[[勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神やニニギに変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。
==関連項目== *[[王子#王子信仰]] *[[九十九王子]]
[[Category:日本の神|にやくいちおうし]] [[Category:修験道の神|にやくいちおうし]]

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、[[神仏習合]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。'''若王子'''（にゃくおうじ）ともいう。
[[熊野三山]]に祀られる[[熊野権現|熊野十二所権現]]は三所権現・五所王子・四所明神に分けられ、若一王子は五所王子の第一位である。若一王子の本地仏は[[十一面観音]]で、[[天照大神]]あるいは[[ニニギ]]と同一視された。[[熊野本宮大社]]・[[熊野速玉大社]]では第4殿、[[熊野那智大社]]では第5殿に祀られる（いずれも、現在は「若宮」と称し、天照大神のこととしている）。
[[熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、熊野権現が各地に[[勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神やニニギに変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。 *[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]][[若王子]]若一王子神社の社叢
==関連項目== *[[王子#王子信仰]] *[[九十九王子]]
[[Category:日本の神|にやくいちおうし]] [[Category:修験道の神|にやくいちおうし]]

*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。 *[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]][[若王子]]若一王子神社の社叢

[[熊野三山]]に祀られる[[熊野権現|熊野十二所権現]]は三所権現・五所王子・四所明神に分けられ、若一王子は五所王子の第一位である。若一王子の本地仏は[[十一面観音]]で、[[天照大神]]あるいは[[ニニギ|瓊々杵尊]]と同一視された。[[熊野本宮大社]]・[[熊野速玉大社]]では第4殿、[[熊野那智大社]]では第5殿に祀られる（いずれも、現在は「若宮」と称し、天照大神のこととしている）。
[[熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、熊野権現が各地に[[勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神や瓊々杵尊に変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
*[[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 *[[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 *[[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 *和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。 *[[静岡県]][[藤枝市]][[若王子]]若一王子神社の社叢

* [[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 * [[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 * [[京都市]][[下京区]][[七条通|七条]][[御所ノ内]][[御所ノ内|本町]]の[[若一神社]]。[[西八条殿]]が所在した場所。<ref>[https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000188]、[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/]</ref> * [[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 * 和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「[[若一王子権現社]]」。 * [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]][[若王子]]若一王子神社の社叢
== 関連項目 == * [[王子神]] * [[九十九王子]] {{Reflist}} {{デフォルトソート:にやくいちおうし}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:修験道の神]] [[Category:熊野信仰]]

[[熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、熊野権現が各地に[[勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を[[天照大神]]や瓊々杵尊に変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。

'''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、[[神仏習合]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。'''若王子'''（にゃくおうじ）ともいう。
[[熊野三山]]に祀られる[[熊野権現|熊野十二所権現]]は三所権現・五所王子・四所明神に分けられ、若一王子は五所王子の第一位である。若一王子の本地仏は[[十一面観音]]で、[[天照大神]]あるいは[[ニニギ|瓊々杵尊]]と同一視された。[[熊野本宮大社]]・[[熊野速玉大社]]では第4殿、[[熊野那智大社]]では第5殿に祀られる（いずれも、現在は「[[若宮]]」と称し、天照大神のこととしている）。
[[熊野三山#熊野信仰|熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、[[熊野権現]]が各地に[[分霊#勧請|勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神や瓊々杵尊に変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
* [[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 * [[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。[[紀伊国]]より勧請。 * [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]][[若王子 (藤枝市)|若王子]]および[[藤枝|藤枝五丁目]]の[[若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|若一王子神社]]の社叢。 * [[京都市]][[左京区]][[若王子町]]の[[熊野若王子神社]]。 * [[京都市]][[下京区]][[七条通|七条]][[御所ノ内]][[御所ノ内|本町]]の[[若一神社]]。[[西八条殿]]が所在した場所<ref>[https://kanko.city.kyoto.lg.jp/detail.php?InforKindCode=1&ManageCode=1000188]、[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/]</ref>。 * [[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、[[若一王子権現碑]]が跡地にある。 * 和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古名「若一王子権現社」。 * [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]][[熊野町 (西宮市)|熊野町]]の[[熊野神社 (西宮市)|熊野神社]]は旧称が「若王子神社」。 * 兵庫県[[加古川市]][[平荘町]]の[[平之荘神社]]（平の荘大宮若一王子権現）。
== 関連項目 == * [[九十九王子]] * [[王子神]] * [[特別:Prefixindex/若一|若一で始まる項目の一覧]] * [[特別:Prefixindex/若王|若王で始まる項目の一覧]]

revision.comment: [[Special:Contributions/スティクス|スティクス]] ([[User talk:スティクス|会話]]) による版を MAYUTO RYOUTA による版へ[[H:RV|巻き戻し]]

[[熊野三山]]に祀られる[[熊野権現|熊野十二所権現]]は三所権現・五所王子・四所明神に分けられ、若一王子は五所王子の第一位である。若一王子の本地仏は[[十一面観音]]で、[[天照大神]]あるいは[[ニニギ|瓊々杵尊]]と同一視された。[[熊野本宮大社]]・[[熊野速玉大社]]では第4殿、[[熊野那智大社]]では第5殿に祀られる（いずれも、現在は「[[若宮]]」と称し、天照大神のこととしている）。
[[熊野三山#熊野信仰|熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、[[熊野権現]]が各地に[[分霊#勧請|勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神や瓊々杵尊に変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
* [[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 * [[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。那智大社第5殿に祀られる「若一王子」を勧請。 * [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]若王子および藤枝五丁目の[[若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|若一王子神社]]の社叢。 * [[京都市]][[左京区]]若王子町の[[熊野若王子神社]]。[[後白河天皇|後白河上皇]]が熊野権現を勧請。別称が「若王子神社」「若王子さん」<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|title=若王子神社|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|accessdate=2019-12-19}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|title=若王子神社|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|accessdate=2019-12-19}}</ref>。 * 京都市[[下京区]][[七条通|七条]]御所ノ内本町の[[若一神社]]。[[平清盛]]の別邸[[西八条殿]]が所在した場所<ref>[https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会)</ref><ref>[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活)</ref>。 * [[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、「若一王子権現」碑が跡地にある。 * 和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古称が「若一王子権現社」。 * [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]熊野町の[[熊野神社 (西宮市)|熊野神社]]は旧称が「若王子神社」。 * 兵庫県[[加古川市]][[平荘町]]の[[平之荘神社]]の古称が「平の荘若一王子権現」。
== 関連項目 == * [[九十九王子]] * [[王子神]] * [[特別:Prefixindex/若一|若一で始まる項目の一覧]] * [[特別:Prefixindex/若王|若王で始まる項目の一覧]]

{{RFD notice|'''対象リダイレクト:'''[[Wikipedia:リダイレクトの削除依頼/受付#RFD若王山|若王山（受付依頼）]]|※編集上のご注意！このテンプレートはBotによって自動貼付、および審議終了後およそ1週間で自動除去されます。審議途中でリダイレクト先を変更した場合や記事に置き換えた場合、またリダイレクトが削除された後でもテンプレートを取り除かないでください。前後に使用されているnoinclude文も同様です。手動で取り除いた場合、再度自動貼付されることがあります。}} '''若一王子'''（にゃくいちおうじ）は、[[神仏習合]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]である。'''若王子'''（にゃくおうじ）ともいう。

[[熊野三山#熊野信仰|熊野信仰]]が日本各地に広まるにつれ、[[熊野権現]]が各地に[[分霊#勧請|勧請]]されたが、若一王子のみを勧請する場合も多かった。[[明治]]の[[神仏分離]]に伴い、「若一王子」を天照大神や瓊々杵尊に変えた所も多いが、従前のまま「若一王子」として祀っている神社もある。
* [[東京都]][[北区 (東京都)|北区]]の[[王子神社 (東京都北区)|王子神社]]。 * [[長野県]][[大町市]]の[[若一王子神社]]。那智大社第5殿に祀られる「若一王子」を勧請。 * [[静岡県]][[藤枝市]]若王子および藤枝五丁目の[[若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|若一王子神社]]の社叢。 * [[京都市]][[左京区]]若王子町の[[熊野若王子神社]]。[[後白河天皇|後白河上皇]]が熊野権現を勧請。別称が「若王子神社」「若王子さん」<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|title=若王子神社|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|accessdate=2019-12-19}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|title=若王子神社|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|accessdate=2019-12-19}}</ref>。 * 京都市[[下京区]][[七条通|七条]]御所ノ内本町の[[若一神社]]。[[平清盛]]の別邸[[西八条殿]]が所在した場所<ref>[https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会)</ref><ref>[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活)</ref>。 * [[和歌山県]][[田辺市]]の神社。現存せず、「若一王子権現」碑が跡地にある。 * 和歌山県[[西牟婁郡]][[すさみ町]]の[[周参見王子神社]]の古称が「若一王子権現社」。 * [[兵庫県]][[西宮市]]熊野町の[[熊野神社 (西宮市)|熊野神社]]は旧称が「若王子神社」。 * 兵庫県[[加古川市]][[平荘町]]の[[平之荘神社]]の古称が「平の荘若一王子権現」。

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/98258516|若一王子]]"
revision.text:
{{ill|Nyakuichiōji|lt=Nyakuichiōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji}} (若一王子 ) is a part of {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}. Identified with {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}. Worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichiōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社}}
'''Nyakuichioji''' is [[Kami|a deity]] in [[:ja:神仏習合|the syncretic Shinto-Buddhist sect]] . He is also called '''Nyakuoji''' .
[[:ja:熊野権現|The Twelve Kumano Gongen deities]] enshrined in [[:ja:熊野三山|the Kumano Sanzan]] are divided into Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin, with Wakaichi Oji being the first of the Gosho Oji. Prince Wakai's original Buddha is [[:ja:十一面観音|the Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is identified with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] or [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] . She is enshrined in the fourth shrine at [[:ja:熊野本宮大社|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:ja:熊野速玉大社|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth shrine at [[:ja:熊野那智大社|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] (both are now called " [[:ja:若宮|Wakamiya]] " and refer to Amaterasu Omikami).
As [[:ja:熊野三山|the belief in Kumano]] spread throughout Japan, [[:ja:熊野権現|Kumano Gongen]] were enshrined in various places, but in many cases only Wakaichi no Oji was enshrined. Following [[:ja:神仏分離|the separation of Shinto and Buddhism]] in [[Meiji period|the Meiji period]], many shrines changed "Wakaichi no Oji" to Amaterasu Omikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, but some shrines continue to worship him as "Wakaichi no Oji" as before.
* [[:ja:王子神社_(東京都北区)|Oji Shrine]] in [[Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * [[:ja:若一王子神社|Wakaichi Oji Shrine]] in [[:ja:大町市|Omachi City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of [[若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine]] in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * [[:ja:熊野若王子神社|Kumano Wakaoji Shrine]] in [[:ja:左京区|Wakaoji-]] cho, Sakyo Ward, [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . [[:ja:後白河天皇|Emperor Goshirakawa]] enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" <ref>{{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}}</ref> <ref>{{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}}</ref> . * [[:ja:若一神社|Wakai Shrine]] in Honmachi [[:ja:七条通|, Shichijo Goshonouchi,]] [[:ja:下京区|Shimogyo Ward]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira [[:ja:平清盛|no Kiyomori's villa, Nishihachijodono]] [[西八条殿|.]] <ref>[https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会)</ref> <ref>[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活)</ref> * A shrine in [[:ja:田辺市|Tanabe City]] [[Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Wakaichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of [[:ja:周参見王子神社|Susami Oji Shrine]] in [[:ja:すさみ町|Susami Town]], [[:ja:西牟婁郡|Nishimuro]] District, Wakayama Prefecture was "Wakaichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * [[:ja:熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano Shrine]] in Kumano-cho, [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[Nishinomiya, Hyōgo|City,]] Hyogo Prefecture was formerly known as Wakaoji Shrine. * The ancient name of [[:ja:平之荘神社|Hiranosho Shrine]] in [[:ja:平荘町|Hirasho-cho]], [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyogo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * [[若一王子神社 (高松市)|Wakaicho Oji Shrine (Takamatsu City)]] - [[Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[Kagawa Prefecture]] * [[:ja:若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Wakaicho Oji Shrine (So City)]] - So City, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]
== footnote == {{Reflist}}
== Related item ==
* [[:ja:九十九王子|99 Princes]] * [[:ja:王子神|Prince God]] * 「若一」で始まるページの一覧 * 「若王」で始まるページの一覧 <nowiki> [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]]</nowiki>

{{ill|Nyakuichiōji|lt=Nyakuichiōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji}} (若一王子[[ja:若一王子]]) is a part of {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}. Identified with {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}. Worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichiōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社}}
[[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichiōji]][[en:draft:Nyakuichiōji]]
'''Nyakuichioji''' is [[Kami|a deity]] in [[:ja:神仏習合|the syncretic Shinto-Buddhist sect]] . He is also called '''Nyakuoji''' .
[[:ja:熊野権現|The Twelve Kumano Gongen deities]] enshrined in [[:ja:熊野三山|the Kumano Sanzan]] are divided into Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin, with Wakaichi Oji being the first of the Gosho Oji. Prince Wakai's original Buddha is [[:ja:十一面観音|the Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is identified with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] or [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] . She is enshrined in the fourth shrine at [[:ja:熊野本宮大社|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:ja:熊野速玉大社|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth shrine at [[:ja:熊野那智大社|Kumano Nachi Taisha]] (both are now called " [[:ja:若宮|Wakamiya]] " and refer to Amaterasu Omikami).
As [[:ja:熊野三山|the belief in Kumano]] spread throughout Japan, [[:ja:熊野権現|Kumano Gongen]] were enshrined in various places, but in many cases only Wakaichi no Oji was enshrined. {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|The Twelve Kumano Gongen deities}} enshrined in {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|the Kumano Sanzan}} are divided into Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin, with Wakaichi Oji being the first of the Gosho Oji. Prince Wakai's original Buddha is {{ill|10=ja|11=十一面観音|the Eleven-Headed Kannon}}, who is identified with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] or [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]] . She is enshrined in the fourth shrine at {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野本宮大社|Kumano Hongu Taisha}} and {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野速玉大社|Kumano Hayatama Taisha}}, and in the fifth shrine at {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野那智大社|Kumano Nachi Taisha}} (both are now called " {{ill|10=ja|11=若宮|Wakamiya}}" and refer to Amaterasu Omikami).
As {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|the belief in Kumano}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr||en:draft}} were enshrined in various places, but in many cases only Wakaichi no Oji was enshrined. Following {{ill|10=ja|11=神仏分離|the separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} in [[Meiji period|the Meiji period]], many shrines changed "Wakaichi no Oji" to Amaterasu Omikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, but some shrines continue to worship him as "Wakaichi no Oji" as before.
* {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神社_(東京都北区)|Ōji Shrine|en}} in [[Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichiōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=大町市|Omachi City}}, [[Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Wakaoji Shrine}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=左京区|Wakaoji-cho}}, Sakyo Ward, [[Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=後白河天皇|Emperor Goshirakawa}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" <ref>{{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}}</ref> <ref>{{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}}</ref> . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Wakai Shrine}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijo Goshonouchi}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=下京区|Shimogyo Ward}}, Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=平清盛|no Kiyomori's villa, Nishihachijodono}} [[西八条殿|.]] <ref>[https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会)</ref> <ref>[http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活)</ref> * A shrine in {{ill|10=ja|11=田辺市|Tanabe City}} [[Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Wakaichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Oji Shrine}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=すさみ町|Susami Town}}, {{ill|10=ja|11=西牟婁郡|Nishimuro}} District, Wakayama Prefecture was "Wakaichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano Shrine}} in Kumano-cho, [[Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[Nishinomiya, Hyōgo|City,]] Hyogo Prefecture was formerly known as Wakaoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Hiranosho Shrine}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Hirasho-cho}}, [[Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyogo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * [[若一王子神社 (高松市)|Wakaicho Oji Shrine (Takamatsu City)]] - [[Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Wakaicho Oji Shrine (So City)}} - So City, [[Kagoshima Prefecture]]

revision.text: {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|user:immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]]

[http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|user:immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]]" to "[[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:"
revision.text: {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|user:immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]]

In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]]

revision.text: {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]]

Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[en:Nyakuichi Ōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text: {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]][[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[sn:yakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}
