This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}

revision.text: {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:Ōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}

Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|qid=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|qid=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:Ōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:Ōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}}

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[:en:Takamatsu|Takamatsu City,]] [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]] * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, [[:en:Kagoshima Prefecture|Kagoshima Prefecture]] == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[Category:Ōji]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō-ku]], Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in [[:en:Tanabe, Wakayama|Tanabe]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|, Wakayama Prefecture]] . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami-ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Susami, Wakayama|Susami]], [[:en:Nishimuro District, Wakayama|Nishimuro District, Wakayama]] was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Nishinomiya]] [[:en:Nishinomiya|City,]] Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa City]], Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11616186]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ouji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|qq=}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and part of {{ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|qq=}}. She is identified with [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:en:Kumano Nachi Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "[[:en:Wakamiya|Wakamiya]]," symbolizing [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to Amaterasu Ōmikami or Ninigi no Mikoto, though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. She is identified with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! The god comes from a lineage of [[:simple:Bunrei|bunrei]] coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," symbolizing [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * [[:en:Ōji Shrine|Ōji Shrine]] in [[:en:Kita, Tokyo|Kita Ward]], [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Ōmachi, Nagano|Ōmachi]], [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]] . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, [[:en:Fujieda, Shizuoka|Fujieda City]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]] . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]] . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen.
