[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. Her [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}} During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. {| class="wikitable" | rowspan="3" |Three [[Gongen]] | |- |- |- | rowspan="5" |Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}} | |- |- |- |- |- | rowspan="4" |Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}} | |- |- |- |} == Old content? == In the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Sanzan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, the Twelve Kumano Deities are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichi Ōji holds the premier position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji’s Buddhist Prototype (本地仏) is the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}}, who is equated with [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. As the {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野三山|Kumano faith|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kumano Sanzan|WD=Q10403700}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野権現|Kumano Gongen|fr|en:draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only Nyakuichi Ōji. Following the Meiji era {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, many shrines shifted their focus from "Nyakuichi Ōji" to [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as "Nyakuichi Ōji" as before. == Simple content == In the {{ill|Kumano Sanzan|10=ja|11=熊野三山}}, the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr|lt=Twelve Kumano gods}} are divided into three groups: Sansho Gongen, Gosho Oji, and Shisho Myojin. Nyakuichiōji holds the top position among the Gosho Oji. Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Nyakuichi Ōji's [[:simple:Honji_Suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (本地仏) is the [[:simple:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-Headed Kannon]], who is equated with [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]. As the {{ill|Kumano faith|10=ja|11=熊野三山}} spread throughout Japan, {{ill|Kumano Gongen|fr||simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|10=ja|11=熊野権現}} shrines were established in various locations, often enshrining only {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}}. Following the Meiji era {{ill|separation of Shinto and Buddhism|10=ja|11=神仏分離}}, many shrines shifted their focus from {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} to [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] or [[:simple:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi no Mikoto]], though some continue to venerate him as {{nihongo|Nyakuichi Ōji|若一王子}} as before. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City,}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == footnote == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]]

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. She is considered the Japanese version of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}}. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. Many shrines in the middle ages were only dedicated to her and no other members of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. But some shrines kept their names. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[:en:Tokyo Metropolis|Tokyo]] . * Wakaichi Oji Shrine in Anjo City, Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Wakaichioji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Wakaichi Oji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu City)|12=|13=|ja_comment=}} - [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji|ja|若一王子|en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji|lt=Nyakuichi Ōji}} (若一王子) is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. She is considered the Japanese version of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}}. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. Many shrines in the middle ages were only dedicated to her and no other members of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. This was the location of Taira {{ill|10=ja|11=Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Nishihachijodono [[西八条殿|.]] [https://ja.kyoto.travel/tourism/single02.php?Category_id=7&tourism_id=438 若一神社].京都観光Navi (京都市観光協会) [http://kyoto.wakasa.jp/detail/25/1772/ 若一神社].ほっこり京都生活 (わかさ生活) * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|WD=Q134989408}} - {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City,}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]]

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{nihongo|'''Nyakuichi Ōji'''|若一王子}} is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. She is considered the Japanese version of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}}. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. Many shrines in the middle ages were only dedicated to her and no other members of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. But some shrines kept their names. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Fujieda Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|12=|13=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q117197847}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}} . {{ill|10=ja|11=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|Emperor Go-Shirakawa|1=Emperor Go-Shirakawa|2=ja|3=後白河天皇|WD=Q317984|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined Kumano Gongen. It is also known as "Wakaoji Shrine" or "Wakaoji-san" {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kyoto-design.jp/spot/3148|access-date=2019-12-19|website=KYOTO-design|publisher=k-iweb|和書}} {{Cite web|title=若王子神社|url=https://kotobank.jp/word/%E8%8B%A5%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE-110362|access-date=2019-12-19|website=コトバンク|publisher=朝日新聞社・VOYAGE MARKETING|和書}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=|13=|qq=|WD=Q123115002}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|WD=Q134989408}} - {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City,}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]]

But some shrines kept their names. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Fujieda Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|12=|13=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q117197847}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}. * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=|13=|qq=|WD=Q123115002}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|WD=Q134989408}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]]

revision.comment: adding dummy category to make categories work
revision.text: [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{nihongo|'''Nyakuichi Ōji'''|若一王子}} is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. She is considered the Japanese version of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon}}. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. Many shrines in the middle ages were only dedicated to her and no other members of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. But some shrines kept their names. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward}}, [[Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Fujieda Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|12=|13=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q117197847}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}. * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=|13=|qq=|WD=Q123115002}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku}}, Kyoto City. * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|WD=Q134989408}} - {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City,}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:pages]]

So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]] [[Category:pages]]

[[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]][[ja:若一王子]][[zh:若一王子]] {{Infobox deity|caption={{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}|equivalent5_type=Japanese |equivalent5={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Eleven-Faced Avalokiteśvara|WD=Q11404731|en|Ekādaśamukha|1=Ekādaśamukha}}|image=Nyakuichiouji jinja-1.jpg}} {{nihongo|'''Nyakuichi Ōji'''|若一王子}} is a syncretic Shinto-Buddhist deity and one of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. People say the is the same as [[:simple:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], and she is worshipped at {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|||fr|Nyakuichiōji-jinja|ja|若一王子神社}}.{{Cite web|title=Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival {{!}} Festivals {{!}} History and Culture {{!}} Trip Ideas {{!}} Go! Nagano|url=https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586|access-date=2025-09-19|website=Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト|language=en}}[https://db.go-nagano.net/en/topics_detail6/id=16586 "Omachi Nyakuichi-Oji Festival | Festivals | History and Culture | Trip Ideas | Go! Nagano"]. ''Go NAGANO 長野県公式観光サイト''. Retrieved 2025-09-19. She is considered the Japanese version of {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}}. The god comes from a lineage of {{Interlanguage link|bunrei|en|bunrei}} coming from {{ill|Hama Ōji Shrine|ja|浜王子|en|qq=}} and eventually {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|simple|User:Immanuelle/Kumano Gongen|fr}}.{{Cite web|last=JapanThis.com|first=marky star-|date=2015-10-04|title=What does Oji mean?|url=http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/|access-date=2025-06-02|website=JAPAN THIS!|language=en-US}}JapanThis.com, marky star- (2015-10-04). [http://japanthis.com/2015/10/04/what-does-oji-mean/ "What does Oji mean?"]. ''JAPAN THIS!''. Retrieved 2025-06-02. Many shrines in the middle ages were only dedicated to her and no other members of the {{Ill|Kumano Gongen|ja|熊野権現||lt=Twelve Kumano gods}}. This deity is enshrined in the fourth hall at both [[:simple:Kumano_Hongu_Taisha|Kumano Hongu Taisha]] and [[:simple:Kumano_Hayatama_Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]], and in the fifth hall at [[:simple:Kumano_Nachi_Taisha|Kumano Nachi Taisha]], all of which are currently referred to as "{{ill|Wakamiya|10=ja|11=若宮}}," There are three groups of deities in the Kumano Gongen: Three [[Gongen]], Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}, and Four {{ill|Myōjin|en|Myōjin|WD=Q5369590|lt=Myōjin|1=Myōjin}}. She is the top member of the Five {{ill|Ōji-no-Kami|ja|王子神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōji-no-Kami|lt=Ōji}}. During the [[Meiji Restoration]] and the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} most shrines that worshipped her changed the listed name to [[Amaterasu]]. But some shrines kept their names. == Shrines == * {{ill|Ōji Shrine|en|Ōji Shrine|WD=Q11573004|lt=Ōji Shrine|1=Ōji Shrine}} in {{ill|Kita, Tokyo|en|Kita, Tokyo|WD=Q235130|lt=Kita Ward|1=Kita, Tokyo}}, [[Tokyo]]. * {{ill|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|simple|User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|en:Draft|Nyakuichi_Ouji_Shrine|ja|若一王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Ōmachi, Nagano|en|Ōmachi, Nagano|WD=Q385375|lt=Ōmachi|1=Ōmachi, Nagano}}, {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}} . The shrine is dedicated to Prince Wakaiichi, who is enshrined in the 5th hall of Nachi Taisha Shrine. * The shrine grove of {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (藤枝市)|Fujieda Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine|12=|13=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q117197847|1=Nyakuichioji Shrine (Fujieda)}} in Wakaoji and Fujieda 5-chome, {{ill|Fujieda, Shizuoka|en|Fujieda, Shizuoka|WD=Q734432|lt=Fujieda City|1=Fujieda, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} . * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野若王子神社|Kumano Nyakuōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一神社|Nyakuichi Shrine|12=|13=|qq=|WD=Q123115002|1=Nyakuichi Shrine}} in Honmachi, {{ill|10=ja|11=七条通|Shichijō Street|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Shichijō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353577}}, {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō-ku|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, Kyoto City. * A shrine in {{ill|Tanabe, Wakayama|en|Tanabe, Wakayama|WD=Q304266|lt=Tanabe|1=Tanabe, Wakayama}} {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=, Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} . It no longer exists, but a monument to "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen" stands at the site. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=周参見王子神社|Susami Ōji Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in {{ill|Susami, Wakayama|en|Susami, Wakayama|WD=Q1346937|lt=Susami|1=Susami, Wakayama}}, {{ill|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|en|Nishimuro District, Wakayama|WD=Q1131323|lt=Nishimuro District, Wakayama|1=Nishimuro District, Wakayama}} was "Nyakuichi Oji Gongen Shrine." * {{ill|10=ja|11=熊野神社_(西宮市)|Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano shrine|lt=Kumano Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} in Kumano-cho, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Nishinomiya|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Nishinomiya|en|Nishinomiya|WD=Q243853|lt=City,|1=Nishinomiya}} Hyōgo Prefecture was formerly known as Nyakuoji Shrine. * The ancient name of {{ill|10=ja|11=平之荘神社|Heinosō-jinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heinosō Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120257}} in {{ill|10=ja|11=平荘町|Heisocho|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|1=Heisocho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215850}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa City|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Hyōgo Prefecture is "Hira no Sho Nyakuichi Ōji Gongen." * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社 (高松市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (Takamatsu)|12=|13=|ja_comment=|WD=Q134989408|1=Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine}} - {{ill|Takamatsu|en|Takamatsu|WD=Q200707|lt=Takamatsu City,|1=Takamatsu}} {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture|1=Kagawa Prefecture}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=若一王子神社_(曽於市)|Nyakuichi Ōji Shrine (So City)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} - So City, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|WD=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}} == See Also == * {{ill|10=ja|11=九十九王子|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|fr|Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji|1=Ninety-Nine Ōji Shrines|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninety-Nine Ōji|lt=Ninety-Nine Ōji|qq=}} * {{ill|10=ja|11=王子神|Ōji-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/10=ja|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} * {{translated page|ja|若一王子|version=98258516|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Nyakuichiōji]] [[zh:%E8%8B%A5%E4%B8%80%E7%8E%8B%E5%AD%90]] [[ja:若一王子]] [[fr:Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[fr:User:Immanuelle/Nyakuichi Ōji]] [[zh:若一王子]] [[zh:若一王子]] {{wikidata link|Q11616186}} [[Category:Japanese deities]] [[Category:Kumano faith]] [[Category:Shugendō deities]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Ōji]]
